description_long: This object is a large, multi-barreled rotary rifle resembling a minigun or Gatling gun, featuring six parallel barrels arranged in a cylindrical cluster. The rear section is dominated by a circular housing with radial fins and a visible belt or cable, likely for ammunition or power. The main body and barrels are constructed from dark, metallic materials, with some reddish-brown accents on the rear housing. The overall design is robust and industrial, with a heavy-duty appearance suitable for urban defense or vehicle mounting. The object lacks a traditional stock and magazine, instead relying on external feed mechanisms. Its imposing size and construction suggest it is intended for high-volume, sustained fire.
description: A large, six-barreled rotary rifle with a circular rear housing and industrial metallic construction, resembling a minigun. Features include a visible belt or cable and robust, heavy-duty design.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the circular rear housing, radial fins, and the reddish-brown accent, with the barrels extending forward.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the back of the circular housing, visible cable, and the full length of the barrel cluster.
description_view_2: Direct rear view focusing on the alignment of the six barrels and the symmetry of the rear housing.
description_view_3: Front-right view emphasizing the face of the circular housing and the clustered barrels extending outward.
